item.title: BT’s turnaround is in the home stretch
item.description: A streamlined domestic business and the end of a hefty investment programme mean it will soon start to generate cash

item.title: Comcast’s Hollywood break-up marks its GE moment
item.description: Owning glamour businesses might be fun for the CEO and board but is not necessarily useful for shareholders

item.title: Burnham’s radical localism alone won’t fix the UK economy
item.description: His speech hits the right political notes but fails to offer a clear roadmap for growth

item.title: Supreme Court protects the Federal Reserve, but what about everybody else?
item.description: While the central bank’s independence was upheld, other agencies were not so lucky

item.title: Argentina’s warning for Venezuela on debt restructuring
item.description: Caracas must secure the backing of the IMF in order to rebuild investor confidence

item.title: Burnham’s devolution plan fits the corporate zeitgeist
item.description: Centrepiece of prime minister-in-waiting’s speech involves a strategy most executives would approve of

item.title: Burnham’s Middle England challenge on devolution
item.description: A boost for town centres and cities against the suburbs could come back to bite him

item.title: Head to tail: business grapples with 100 per cent fish initiative
item.description: An ancient habit of frugality resonates with the US Midwest culture of thrift

item.title: Donald Trump and how strongman leaders fall
item.description: The downfalls of would-be autocrats in Hungary, Brazil and the Philippines hold lessons for America

item.title: Britain’s case for meaningful devolution is overwhelming
item.description: Andy Burnham is right to argue for a shift in power but he has to manage political risk

item.title: AInflation is real but tiny
item.description: Plus, European stocks ain’t cheap

item.title: The ONS: an Outrage of National Significance
item.description: The UK statistics agency plays a vital role but has been beset by errors and poor leadership

item.title: The new AI-based world order
item.description: A single factor is now dictating the hierarchy of global returns

item.title: The leap of faith behind SpaceX’s mega bond deal
item.description: The views of equity and bond investors might one day be at odds with each other

item.title: This is a cancel culture we can use. Let the real hot air do our talking
item.description: A behind-the-scenes look at the work of Rutherford Hall, critical communications strategist

item.title: Don’t allow Congo’s leaders to trample its constitution
item.description: The US should not look the other way as President Tshisekedi seeks an illegal third term

item.title: Ukraine’s vibe shift is bad news for Russia’s economy
item.description: As Kyiv’s battlefield fortunes change, policies long favoured by the west could now do real damage to Moscow

item.title: Germany’s important pension reform plan
item.description: An ambitious move that could have positive Europe-wide implications

item.title: Robots, not chatbots, will realise AI’s potential
item.description: Factory-floor applications of the technology could significantly enhance rich-world economies

item.title: Why keeping Europe cool need not be a luxury
item.description: In the clean energy system the continent is trying to build, air conditioning basically comes for free

item.title: So, about that saga of Trump’s reflecting pool . . .
item.description: In a desperate bid to deflect attention, the president is doing what he loves to do: blaming the libs

item.title: No, we have not had enough of experts
item.description: Trust in specialists is growing 10 years after Brexit but employers still need more of them

item.title: Why is the Starmer to Burnham transition so long?
item.description: Like St Augustine, the assumed new Labour leader appears to want to be prime minister — but not yet

item.title: Chipmakers are expensive globally
item.description: Valuations are high for AI-exposed markets

item.title: Luxury may be in the doldrums, but perfume passes the smell test
item.description: Fragrances are among the faster-growing areas of the beauty industry — though investing in them isn’t straightforward
